result:
By default, React re-renders every child when the parent re-renders, regardless of whether props changed. That's the baseline behavior, not a bug.

To prevent it:
- Wrap the child in `React.memo(Component)` — it will skip re-renders when props are shallow-equal.
- Make sure the props you pass are stable. Inline objects (`{foo: 1}`), arrays, and functions (`() => ...`) are new references every render, which defeats `memo`. Use `useMemo` / `useCallback` in the parent to stabilize them.
- If the child reads context, it re-renders when that context value changes — `memo` doesn't help there.

Common gotcha: `memo` + `onClick={() => doThing(id)}` — the arrow function is a new reference each parent render, so `memo` always sees "props changed."

If you share the component, I can point at the specific cause.

- Wrap the child in `React.memo(Component)` — it will skip re-renders when props are shallow-equal.
- Make sure the props you pass are stable. Inline objects (`{foo: 1}`), arrays, and functions (`() => ...`) are new references every render, which defeats `memo`. Use `useMemo` / `useCallback` in the parent to stabilize them.
- If the child reads context, it re-renders when that context value changes — `memo` doesn't help there.

Common gotcha: `memo` + `onClick={() => doThing(id)}` — the arrow function is a new reference each parent render, so `memo` always sees "props changed."
